finding: `tasks scope add` can require a claim, while `next --claim` can require scope expansion; the successful escape hatch is `--claim-first`, but the error path and help text do not lead there clearly enough.
expectedBehavior: When claim is blocked by scope expansion, ATM should suggest the exact `tasks scope add --claim-first ...` command. Help text should distinguish task-card scope/deliverables from runtime lock repair.
evidenceOrRepro: Fixed by making no-lock/released-lock scope amendment failures expose `tasks scope add --claim-first --add <paths>` as the primary `requiredCommand`, while retaining `next --claim` as `claimCommand`; regression asserts both human text and structured data expose the claim-first escape hatch. Validator: `node --strip-types tests/cli/tasks-scope-add-error-clarity.test.ts`.
